description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun, visually reminiscent of the Thompson 'Tommy Gun' with a distinctive round drum magazine. The rifle features a wooden fixed stock and foregrip, both with a rich brown finish, contrasting with the matte black metal receiver and barrel. The long, slender barrel extends from the main body, ending in a simple muzzle with no visible attachments. The drum magazine is prominent and centrally located beneath the receiver, giving the weapon a robust and iconic appearance. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the receiver and barrel, providing basic aiming capability. The overall design is utilitarian, with a blend of wood and metal materials typical of early-to-mid 20th-century firearms.
description: A classic submachine gun with a wooden stock and foregrip, black metal body, and a large round drum magazine. Features iron sights and a long, slender barrel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, including the wooden stock, foregrip, drum magazine, and long barrel.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular drum magazine and the alignment of the barrel and sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first but with the stock and foregrip visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock and the back of the receiver, with the drum magazine visible below.
